claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you assess your eligibility for Child Benefit. Let me start by gathering some information about your situation.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit, I'll need to ask you some questions. Remember, Child Benefit eligibility is assessed for each individual child.

Let me start with the first question:

**Do you have a child or children you're responsible for? If yes, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child I'm asking about. My son Alex is 8 years old and lives with me here in Manchester. I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for that information! Alex is 8 years old, which means he's under 16, so that's a good start. Now I need to ask a few more questions to assess your eligibility.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Is Alex in paid work for 24 hours or more per week?**

(I expect the answer is no given his age, but I need to confirm this as part of the assessment.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, of course not! He's only 8, so he's at primary school. He doesn't have any job.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, that makes sense! Now let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: **Is Alex receiving any benefits in his own right, such as Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he doesn't receive any benefits himself. He's just a child.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent, thank you. Let me continue with the next question.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Is Alex in any form of apprenticeship?**

(Again, given his age this is likely not applicable, but I need to verify as part of the assessment.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in an apprenticeship. He's only 8 years old, still in primary school!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Of course, thank you for your patience with these questions! Now I need to ask about your own circumstances as the claimant.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Do you normally live in the UK?**

You mentioned you're in Manchester, so I assume the answer is yes, but I need to confirm this for the assessment.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right. I live in Manchester - I'm a UK resident.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Now I need to ask about your responsibility for Alex.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Do you live with Alex?**

You mentioned he lives with you in Manchester, so I just need to confirm this.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with me at home.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent. Now I need to check if anyone else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: **Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Is Alex currently in any of the following situations:**
- In hospital
- In local authority care
- Being fostered

Or does he live normally at home with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He lives normally at home with me. He's not in hospital, care, or being fostered.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: **Is the local council paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, the council isn't paying anything towards Alex. I'm supporting him myself.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent news! Based on all the information you've provided, **you are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's a summary of why you're eligible:
- Alex is 8 years old (under 16), so he's a qualifying child
- He's not in paid work, receiving benefits in his own right, or in an apprenticeship
- You normally live in the UK (Manchester)
- You are responsible for Alex as he lives with you
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him
- He lives normally at home with you (not in hospital, care, or fostered)
- The local council is not paying towards his accommodation or maintenance

**Next steps to claim:**
- You can apply online or by post using form CH2
- You'll need to provide proof of identity for yourself and Alex
- You'll need Alex's birth certificate or adoption certificate
- Be aware that if you or your partner earns over £60,000, the High Income Child Benefit Charge may apply (though you can still claim)
- Once approved, payments are made every 4 weeks (or weekly if you're a single parent or receiving certain benefits)

Would you like any more information about how to make your claim?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's great news, thank you so much! You've explained everything really clearly. I understand what I need to do now - I'll get Alex's birth certificate ready and apply online. Thanks for your help!
